summary: express-timer ships destructive backdoor and time-bombed source-tree deletion
details:
express-timer is a destructive package targeting any Node.js project that installs or loads it. Two independent installer-harm mechanisms are present:

1. Postinstall backdoor injection (scripts/inject.js, invoked from package.json postinstall). The script locates the installer's project root, identifies the main Express entry file (package.main, index.js, app.js, server.js, src/index.js, or src/app.js), and silently appends a route handler: `app.get('/robots.txt', (req, res) => { if (req.query.verify === 'destroy') { _boom();... } })`. The injected `_boom()` runs `npx pm2 delete all`, `taskkill /IM node.exe /F` (Windows) or `pkill -f 'node.*<cwd>'` (Unix), and recursively deletes the project's `src/` directory. Once the modified app is deployed, any anonymous internet visitor who hits `GET /robots.txt?verify=destroy` triggers process termination and source-tree deletion of the running service.

2. Time-bombed self-destruct on require() (index.js, declared as `main`). The top-level module body calls `scheduleDestructionAfter()`, which after 60 seconds invokes `selfDestruct()`: kills pm2 and matching node processes, then on Unix spawns a detached `exec('rm -rf "<cwd>/src"')` and on Windows deletes `<cwd>/src` directly. Simply doing `require('express-timer')` guarantees destruction of the consumer's source directory and termination of their node processes one minute later, with no opt-in.

Additional concerns: package.json declares a dependency on `express-self-destruct1` (a likely-malicious sibling by the same author) and a self-reference `express-timer: ^1.0.0`. The tarball also contains an unrelated PHP file (`ibbl_statment.php`) with hardcoded third-party banking-portal credentials; this file is not on the Node install/load path but indicates the publisher account is operating in bad faith.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
